pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'David Nakamura'; verifier.py _find_patient() checks firstName.lower() == 'david'; setup_task.sh cleanup targets 'david' + 'nakamura'

Task description explicitly states 'David Nakamura'; verifier.py _find_patient() checks lastName.lower() == 'nakamura'; setup_task.sh cleanup targets 'nakamura'. Note: pre-seeded patient 'Linda Nakamura' exists in CouchDB (patient_p1_000007) — setup_task.sh was specifically updated to only match 'David Nakamura' (not just 'Nakamura') to avoid deleting Linda's record.

Task description states 'DOB 03/15/1974'; verifier.py checks '1974' in dateOfBirth field. HospitalRun stores dates in MM/DD/YYYY format. Patient age (52 in 2026) is consistent with the clinical scenario.
Task description states '52-year-old male'; verifier.py checks sex.lower() == 'male' or 'm'. HospitalRun registration form has Sex dropdown with 'Male' option.
Task description states 'Blood Type A+'; verifier.py checks 'a+' in bloodType.lower(). HospitalRun registration form has Blood Type dropdown.
Task description states 'Phone 617-555-0342'; verifier.py checks '617' and '0342' both present in phone field. Uses 555 prefix (standard fictional US phone number convention).

Task description states this address; verifier.py checks any of 'elm', '2847', or 'boston' in address.lower(). Address is fictional (no real 2847 Elm Street in Boston 02108 zip), consistent with synthetic patient data convention.

Verifier.py VISIT_TYPE_KEYWORDS constant includes 'er ' and 'ed ' (with trailing space) which are absent from metadata. Task description says 'create an admission visit'. The verifier accepts a broader set of visit type keywords than metadata claims.
Task description states 'Location: Emergency Department'; verifier.py checks 'emergency' in doc_str (substring match). HospitalRun visit form has a Location text field.

Task description states 'Examiner: Dr. Sarah Mitchell'. Note: verifier.py does NOT check the examiner field — it only verifies visit type and location. The examiner is part of the task requirement but not programmatically verified.

Task description states 'BP 168/98'; verifier.py uses metadata.get('bp_systolic', '168') and checks str(168) in doc_str. Clinically plausible: Stage 2 hypertension consistent with ACS + known hypertension (PMC8030564).
Task description states 'BP 168/98'; verifier.py uses metadata.get('bp_diastolic', '98'). Clinically plausible for hypertensive ACS patient.
Task description states 'HR 104'; verifier.py uses metadata.get('heart_rate', '104'). Clinically expected: sinus tachycardia from catecholamine surge in ACS (Medscape ACS, StatPearls NBK459157).
Task description states 'RR 22'; verifier.py uses metadata.get('respiratory_rate', '22'). Clinically plausible: mild tachypnea (>20/min) from pain/anxiety in ACS (StatPearls NBK541062).
Task description states 'Temp 37.2 C'; verifier.py uses metadata.get('temperature', '37.2'). Clinically correct: normal range for early ACS; fever develops 4-48h post-MI (BMC Cardiovasc Disord 2023).

Task description states 'O2 Sat 95%'; verifier.py uses metadata.get('o2_saturation', '95'). Clinically plausible: borderline but above ACC/AHA mandatory supplemental O2 threshold of <90% (2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline).
Task description states 'Weight 91kg'; verifier.py uses metadata.get('weight_kg', '91'). BMI = 91/(1.75^2) = 29.7 (overweight), a cardiac risk factor (AHA Circulation).
Task description states 'Height 175cm'; verifier.py uses metadata.get('height_cm', '175').

Verifier.py DIAGNOSIS_KEYWORDS constant includes 'heart attack', 'stemi', 'nstemi' which are absent from metadata. The verifier accepts a broader set of diagnosis keywords. Task description says 'Add diagnosis Acute Coronary Syndrome'.
Verifier.py LAB_KEYWORDS constant matches metadata exactly. Task description says 'Order a Troponin I lab test'. Troponin I is the gold-standard ACS biomarker (PubMed 16232149).

Verifier.py IMAGING_KEYWORDS constant matches metadata exactly. Task description says 'Chest X-ray (notes: PA and lateral views...)'. CXR is standard ED ACS workup (2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline).
Verifier.py MED_KEYWORDS constant matches metadata exactly. Task description says 'Prescribe Aspirin 325mg'. Aspirin 162-325mg chewable stat is Class I, LOE A per 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline.

Verifier.py APPT_KEYWORDS constant does NOT include 'chest pain' which IS in metadata. Metadata is a superset of verifier keywords. Task description says 'Schedule a Cardiology follow-up appointment... reason Post-ED cardiac risk stratification'. Cardiology follow-up within 1-2 weeks is standard post-ACS protocol (2025 ACC/AHA, ESC, PMC9266793).

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task requiring complete ED admission for patient David Nakamura (52M, DOB 03/15/1974, Male, Blood Type A+, Phone 617-555-0342, Address 2847 Elm Street Boston MA 02108). The task has 8 verifiable sub-objectives scored out of 100 points (pass threshold 50): (1) Patient registration with name David Nakamura and at least 3 of 5 demographics correct (17 pts); (2) Admission/emergency visit with location containing 'Emergency' (12 pts); (3) Vitals with at least 3 of these 8 values present in the document: 168, 98, 104, 22, 37.2, 95, 91, 175 (15 pts); (4) Diagnosis containing any of: 'acute coronary', 'coronary syndrome', 'acs', 'chest pain', 'angina', 'myocardial', 'cardiac', 'ami', 'heart attack', 'stemi', 'nstemi' (12 pts); (5) Lab order containing any of: 'troponin', 'trop', 'cardiac enzyme', 'cardiac marker', 'tnl', 'tni' (12 pts); (6) Imaging order containing any of: 'x-ray', 'xray', 'chest', 'radiograph', 'cxr', 'pa and lateral' (12 pts); (7) Medication order containing any of: 'aspirin', 'asa', 'acetylsalicylic', '325' (10 pts); (8) Appointment with cardiology/cardiac/cardiologist/heart/risk stratification keywords and date containing '2026-04' or '04/0' or '04/1' or 'april' (10 pts). Important: the verifier searches ALL CouchDB documents for 'nakamura' substring to find linked records, so any document mentioning 'nakamura' in any field will be considered. The verifier does NOT check examiner name. Pre-seeded patient Linda Nakamura (patient_p1_000007) exists in the database and must not be confused with the new David Nakamura. The setup navigates to #/patients/edit/new (new patient form). Login credentials are hradmin / test. All clinical values are medically accurate for an ACS presentation per 2025 ACC/AHA guidelines.
